title: Add bin/spine-doctor shell script for pipeline health checks
description:
## Problem

There is no way to verify the spine pipeline is correctly installed and wired. After installing 7+ tools from the tap, a user (or agent) has to manually run each binary to check it exists, then mentally reconstruct the pipeline order from individual --describe outputs.

During the spring11 CRE extraction session, we discovered:
- \`profile --describe\` crashes (exit 2, "Failed to read operator.json")
- \`rvl\` is missing --describe, --schema, and --version entirely
- \`fingerprint --describe\` returns a different schema format than the rest
- The binary name (\`hashbytes\`) doesn't match the describe name (\`hash\`) or the formula name (\`cmdrvl-hash\`)

None of this was obvious until we tried each tool individually.

## Proposal

A shell script at \`bin/spine-doctor\` that:

1. Checks each known binary exists on PATH
2. Runs \`<tool> --version\` and reports version (or flags failure)
3. Runs \`<tool> --describe\` and flags failures or non-standard output
4. Assembles and prints the pipeline DAG from \`pipeline.upstream/downstream\`
5. Flags naming inconsistencies (binary vs describe name)
6. Reports an overall health status

Example output:
\`\`\`
spine-doctor

Tools:
  vacuum     0.1.0  ✓ describe ✓ schema
  hashbytes  0.2.0  ✓ describe ✓ schema  ⚠ name mismatch (describe: "hash")
  fingerprint 0.3.0 ⚠ describe (non-standard schema)  ✓ schema
  shape      0.2.0  ✓ describe  ✗ schema (not supported)
  profile    0.1.0  ✗ describe (exit 2)  ✓ schema
  rvl        ???    ✗ describe  ✗ schema  ✗ version
  lock       0.2.0  ✓ describe ✓ schema
  canon      (not installed)
  pack       (not installed)

Pipeline:
  vacuum → hashbytes → fingerprint → lock
                    ↘ shape → rvl
           profile ─────────→ (shape, rvl) [deferred in v0]

Health: 5/9 tools installed, 2 with --describe issues, 1 missing --version
\`\`\`

## Implementation notes

- Pure shell script, no dependencies beyond the tools themselves
- The tap already defines the canonical formula list — derive tool names from Formula/*.rb
- Could also check witness ledger health (\`~/.epistemic/witness.jsonl\` exists and is writable)
- Keep it simple: if it grows beyond ~100 lines, graduate to a real binary in its own repo
notes: Dependency note: spine-doctor will report issues that are being fixed in parallel (bd-25h rvl --describe, bd-1c4 profile --describe, bd-3mz fingerprint operator.v0, bd-1e0 hash naming). Implement after those fixes land for accurate baseline reporting. Consider P3 until prerequisites are done.
close_reason: Added bin/spine-doctor — checks all 9 tools for PATH presence, --version, --describe (operator.v0), --schema, name mismatches, and witness ledger health. 95 lines of pure bash, no external deps.
